verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase that is myristoylated and palmitoylated at its N-terminus, anchoring it to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane under baseline conditions — it is not a transmembrane protein and has no extracellular domain by its canonical topology. However, multiple lines of evidence support a contextual outer-leaflet pool: (1) Several Src-family kinases, including LYN, have been documented as cancer ecto-kinases displayed on the outer face of tumor cells, constituting a cell-state-induced ecto-pool accessible to extracellular antibodies or kinase inhibitors from outside the cell. (2) LYN is involved in mast cell degranulation and B-cell receptor signaling, contexts where activation-induced redistribution of inner-leaflet pools has been reported. Under baseline non-transformed conditions the canonical localization is inner-leaflet/cytoplasmic, but the documented cancer-cell ecto-display qualifies it as contextual/cell_state_induced rather than a hard no.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Extent and robustness of LYN outer-leaflet ecto-display on tumor cells vs. inner-leaflet-only localization; no dedicated clinical surface-directed therapeutic program confirmed against LYN specifically.
